verses.greek: ἐπ’ ἐσχάτου τῶν ἡμερῶν τούτων ἐλάλησεν ἡμῖν ἐν υἱῷ, ὃν ἔθηκεν κληρονόμον πάντων, δι’ οὗ καὶ ⸂ἐποίησεν τοὺς αἰῶνας⸃·
verses.mistral: In the last days of these, He has spoken to us in a Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the ages.
verses.kjv: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὃς ὢν ἀπαύγασμα τῆς δόξης καὶ χαρακτὴρ τῆς ὑποστάσεως αὐτοῦ, φέρων τε τὰ πάντα τῷ ῥήματι τῆς δυνάμεως, ⸂δι’ αὑτοῦ⸃ καθαρισμὸν ⸂τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ποιησάμενος⸃ ἐκάθισεν ἐν δεξιᾷ τῆς μεγαλωσύνης ἐν ὑψηλοῖς,
verses.mistral: Who being the radiance of glory and the exact representation of His nature, bearing all things by the word of His power, through Him making purification of sins, sat down at the right hand of the majesty on high.
verses.kjv: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;

verses.greek: τοσούτῳ κρείττων γενόμενος τῶν ἀγγέλων ὅσῳ διαφορώτερον παρ’ αὐτοὺς κεκληρονόμηκεν ὄνομα.
verses.mistral: Superior to the angels, He has become, in this respect, inheriting a name.

verses.mistral: Neither angel has ever said: "You are my Son, today I have begotten you," nor, "I will be his Father, and he will be my Son."
verses.kjv: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?

verses.mistral: And to the angels He says: "The one making His angels spirits, and His servants a flame of fire."
verses.kjv: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.

verses.greek: πρὸς δὲ τὸν υἱόν· Ὁ θρόνος σου ὁ θεὸς εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τοῦ αἰῶνος, ⸂καὶ ἡ ῥάβδος τῆς εὐθύτητος⸃ ῥάβδος τῆς βασιλείας ⸀σου.
verses.mistral: Thine (Thou's) throne, O God, is forever and ever: a scepter of righteousness is the scepter of thy kingdom.
verses.kjv: But unto the Son he saith, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.kjv: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

verses.greek: καί· Σὺ κατ’ ἀρχάς, κύριε, τὴν γῆν ἐθεμελίωσας, καὶ ἔργα τῶν χειρῶν σού εἰσιν οἱ οὐρανοί·

verses.mistral: To whom did he ever say, "Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet?"
verses.kjv: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?

verses.mistral: How shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation, which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed to us by those who heard.
verses.kjv: How shall we escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard him;

verses.mistral: Witnessing to God by signs and wonders, various powers, and distributions of the Holy Spirit according to His will.
verses.kjv: God also bearing them witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?

verses.kjv: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.

verses.greek: διεμαρτύρατο δέ πού τις λέγων· Τί ἐστιν ἄνθρωπος ὅτι μιμνῄσκῃ αὐτοῦ, ἢ υἱὸς ἀνθρώπου ὅτι ἐπισκέπτῃ αὐτόν;
verses.mistral: Said something: What is man that You are mindful of him, or the son of man that You care for him?

verses.mistral: You have made him a little lower than the angels, and you have crowned him with glory and honor.
verses.kjv: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:

verses.mistral: We see Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels, crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.
verses.kjv: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.

verses.mistral: It was fitting for him, for whom and through whom all things exist, to bring many sons to glory, making the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
verses.kjv: For it became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.

verses.mistral: Again, I will be confident until him; again, behold, I and the children whom God has given me.

verses.mistral: And he will deliver those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery.
verses.kjv: And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ δήπου ἀγγέλων ἐπιλαμβάνεται, ἀλλὰ σπέρματος Ἀβραὰμ ἐπιλαμβάνεται.

verses.kjv: For verily he took not on him the nature of angels; but he took on him the seed of Abraham.

verses.greek: ὅθεν ὤφειλεν κατὰ πάντα τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς ὁμοιωθῆναι, ἵνα ἐλεήμων γένηται καὶ πιστὸς ἀρχιερεὺς τὰ πρὸς τὸν θεόν, εἰς τὸ ἱλάσκεσθαι τὰς ἁμαρτίας τοῦ λαοῦ·
verses.mistral: He was obligated in every respect to be made like his brothers, so that he could become merciful and faithful as a high priest before God, to expiate the sins of the people.
verses.kjv: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.

verses.kjv: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.

verses.mistral: Holy brothers, participants of the heavenly calling, consider the apostle and high priest of our confession Jesus
verses.kjv: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;

verses.mistral: Faithful to the one who made him, as also Moses was in his house.
verses.kjv: Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses was faithful in all his house.

verses.mistral: Moses was faithful in all his house as a servant for a witness concerning those things to be spoken.
verses.kjv: And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;

verses.kjv: But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.

on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.kjv: Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith, To day if ye will hear his voice,

verses.kjv: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years.

verses.greek: τεσσεράκοντα ἔτη· διὸ προσώχθισα τῇ γενεᾷ ⸀ταύτῃ καὶ εἶπον· Ἀεὶ πλανῶνται τῇ καρδίᾳ· αὐτοὶ δὲ οὐκ ἔγνωσαν τὰς ὁδούς μου·

verses.mistral: See, brothers, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, departing from the living God.

verses.mistral: Harden not your hearts as in the rebellion.
verses.kjv: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.

verses.kjv: For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.

verses.kjv: And having an high priest over the house of God;

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: προσερχώμεθα μετὰ ἀληθινῆς καρδίας ἐν πληροφορίᾳ πίστεως, ῥεραντισμένοι τὰς καρδίας ἀπὸ συνειδήσεως πονηρᾶς καὶ λελουσμένοι τὸ σῶμα ὕδατι καθαρῷ·
verses.mistral: Approach with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having been purified from a guilty conscience, and having been washed with pure water.
verses.kjv: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.

verses.greek: κατέχωμεν τὴν ὁμολογίαν τῆς ἐλπίδος ἀκλινῆ, πιστὸς γὰρ ὁ ἐπαγγειλάμενος·
verses.mistral: Hold firmly the confession of hope unwavering, for He who promised is faithful.
verses.kjv: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)

verses.greek: καὶ κατανοῶμεν ἀλλήλους εἰς παροξυσμὸν ἀγάπης καὶ καλῶν ἔργων,
verses.mistral: Encourage one another to acts of love and good works
verses.kjv: And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:

verses.greek: μὴ ἐγκαταλείποντες τὴν ἐπισυναγωγὴν ἑαυτῶν, καθὼς ἔθος τισίν, ἀλλὰ παρακαλοῦντες, καὶ τοσούτῳ μᾶλλον ὅσῳ βλέπετε ἐγγίζουσαν τὴν ἡμέραν.
verses.mistral: Do not forsake the assembling of yourselves, as is the habit of some, but encourage one another, and all the more as you see the Day approaching.
verses.kjv: Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.

verses.greek: Ἑκουσίως γὰρ ἁμαρτανόντων ἡμῶν μετὰ τὸ λαβεῖν τὴν ἐπίγνωσιν τῆς ἀληθείας, οὐκέτι περὶ ἁμαρτιῶν ἀπολείπεται θυσία,
verses.mistral: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted the heavenly gift, and have become partakers of the Holy Spirit, if they fall away, to renew them again to repentance—since they crucify again for themselves the Son of God, and put Him to open shame. Here's the full verse 10:26-31 for context: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted the heavenly gift, and have become partakers of the Holy Spirit, if they fall away, to renew them again to repentance—since they crucify again for themselves the Son of God, and put Him to open shame. For ground that has drunk the rain which often falls on it and brings forth vegetation useful to those for whose sake it is also tilled, receives a blessing from God; but if it bears thorns and briars, it is rejected and near to being cursed, whose end is to be burned. But, beloved, we are convinced of better things concerning you, things that accompany salvation. For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love which you have shown toward His name, in having ministered and in still ministering to the saints. And we desire that each one of you show the same diligence to the full assurance of hope until the end, so that you may not become sluggish, but imitate those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
verses.kjv: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,

words.gloss: I leave, leave behind, desert, am reserved

verses.greek: φοβερὰ δέ τις ἐκδοχὴ κρίσεως καὶ πυρὸς ζῆλος ἐσθίειν μέλλοντος τοὺς ὑπεναντίους.
verses.mistral: A fearful expectation of judgment and fiery indignation is awaiting those who oppose you.
verses.kjv: But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀθετήσας τις νόμον Μωϋσέως χωρὶς οἰκτιρμῶν ἐπὶ δυσὶν ἢ τρισὶν μάρτυσιν ἀποθνῄσκει·
verses.mistral: Neglected one who dismisses the law of Moses without compassion, dies by two or three witnesses.
verses.kjv: He that despised Moses' law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:

I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: πόσῳ δοκεῖτε χείρονος ἀξιωθήσεται τιμωρίας ὁ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ θεοῦ καταπατήσας, καὶ τὸ αἷμα τῆς διαθήκης κοινὸν ἡγησάμενος ἐν ᾧ ἡγιάσθη, καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς χάριτος ἐνυβρίσας.
verses.mistral: You are incurable and deserving of heavier punishment than those who have suffered under the law, having trampled upon the Son of God, treated the blood of the covenant as common, and insulted the Spirit of grace.
verses.kjv: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?

words.gloss: I think, seem, appear, it seems
I account or treat as worthy

verses.greek: οἴδαμεν γὰρ τὸν εἰπόντα· Ἐμοὶ ἐκδίκησις, ἐγὼ ⸀ἀνταποδώσω· καὶ πάλιν· ⸂Κρινεῖ κύριος⸃ τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: We know the one who spoke, "It is mine to avenge; I will repay." The Lord will judge his people.
verses.kjv: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand

verses.greek: φοβερὸν τὸ ἐμπεσεῖν εἰς χεῖρας θεοῦ ζῶντος.
verses.mistral: A dreadful thing it is to fall into the hands of a living God.
verses.kjv: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

words.gloss: I fall in, am cast in

verses.greek: Ἀναμιμνῄσκεσθε δὲ τὰς πρότερον ἡμέρας, ἐν αἷς φωτισθέντες πολλὴν ἄθλησιν ὑπεμείνατε παθημάτων,
verses.mistral: Remember the former days, in which, being enlightened, you endured a great contest of sufferings
verses.kjv: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τοῦτο μὲν ὀνειδισμοῖς τε καὶ θλίψεσιν θεατριζόμενοι, τοῦτο δὲ κοινωνοὶ τῶν οὕτως ἀναστρεφομένων γενηθέντες·
verses.mistral: Witnessing insults and suffering, we have become companions to those who are thus being brought to perfection.
verses.kjv: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.

words.gloss: I make a public show of
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ τοῖς ⸀δεσμίοις συνεπαθήσατε, καὶ τὴν ἁρπαγὴν τῶν ὑπαρχόντων ὑμῶν μετὰ χαρᾶς προσεδέξασθε, γινώσκοντες ἔχειν ⸀ἑαυτοὺς κρείττονα ⸀ὕπαρξιν καὶ μένουσαν.
verses.mistral: And for the sake of those who were in bonds, you shared sufferings; and, having received with joy the plundering of your belongings, you acknowledged that you had a better possession and one that remains.
verses.kjv: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.

words.gloss: I sympathize with, have compassion on
I am, exist, am in possession
I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: μὴ ἀποβάλητε οὖν τὴν παρρησίαν ὑμῶν, ἥτις ἔχει ⸂μεγάλην μισθαποδοσίαν⸃,
verses.mistral: Do not forsake the unity of your confidence, which has great recompense.
verses.kjv: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.

words.gloss: I throw away from, throw overboard

verses.greek: ὑπομονῆς γὰρ ἔχετε χρείαν ἵνα τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ ποιήσαντες κομίσησθε τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν·
verses.mistral: Endure, for you have need of patience, that the will of God may be done, and thus you may receive the promise.
verses.kjv: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.

verses.greek: ἔτι γὰρ μικρὸν ὅσον ὅσον, ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἥξει καὶ οὐ χρονίσει·
verses.mistral: Not yet a little while, and the one coming will come and will not tarry.
verses.kjv: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ δίκαιός ⸀μου ἐκ πίστεως ζήσεται, καὶ ἐὰν ὑποστείληται, οὐκ εὐδοκεῖ ἡ ψυχή μου ἐν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: The righteous one shall live by faith; and if he is drawn away, my soul has no pleasure in him.
verses.kjv: Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἡμεῖς δὲ οὐκ ἐσμὲν ὑποστολῆς εἰς ἀπώλειαν, ἀλλὰ πίστεως εἰς περιποίησιν ψυχῆς.
verses.mistral: We are not for destruction, but for salvation through faith.
verses.kjv: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἔστιν δὲ πίστις ἐλπιζομένων ὑπόστασις, πραγμάτων ἔλεγχος οὐ βλεπομένων·
verses.mistral: Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
verses.kjv: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐν ταύτῃ γὰρ ἐμαρτυρήθησαν οἱ πρεσβύτεροι.
verses.mistral: In this, the elders have been testified
verses.kjv: For by it the elders obtained a good report.

verses.greek: πίστει νοοῦμεν κατηρτίσθαι τοὺς αἰῶνας ῥήματι θεοῦ, εἰς τὸ μὴ ἐκ φαινομένων ⸂τὸ βλεπόμενον⸃ γεγονέναι.
verses.mistral: We understand that through faith the ages have been brought by the decree of God, so that what is unseen might be seen and revealed.
verses.kjv: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Πίστει πλείονα θυσίαν Ἅβελ παρὰ Κάϊν προσήνεγκεν τῷ θεῷ, δι’ ἧς ἐμαρτυρήθη εἶναι δίκαιος, μαρτυροῦντος ἐπὶ τοῖς δώροις αὐτοῦ ⸂τοῦ θεοῦ⸃, καὶ δι’ αὐτῆς ἀποθανὼν ἔτι ⸀λαλεῖ.
verses.mistral: Through faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain, by which he was testified to be righteous, speaking about his offerings to God, and by this faith he still speaks, though he is dead.
verses.kjv: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: Πίστει Ἑνὼχ μετετέθη τοῦ μὴ ἰδεῖν θάνατον, καὶ οὐχ ηὑρίσκετο διότι μετέθηκεν αὐτὸν ὁ θεός· πρὸ γὰρ τῆς ⸀μεταθέσεως μεμαρτύρηται εὐαρεστηκέναι τῷ θεῷ,
verses.mistral: Through faith, Enoch was translated so as not to see death, and because he was translated, he has not been found. For before his translation, he had been testified to be pleasing to God.
verses.kjv: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.

verses.greek: χωρὶς δὲ πίστεως ἀδύνατον εὐαρεστῆσαι, πιστεῦσαι γὰρ δεῖ τὸν προσερχόμενον τῷ θεῷ ὅτι ἔστιν καὶ τοῖς ἐκζητοῦσιν αὐτὸν μισθαποδότης γίνεται.
verses.mistral: Without faith, it is impossible to please God, for he who approaches Him must believe that He is and that He is a rewarder of those who seek Him.
verses.kjv: But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: πίστει χρηματισθεὶς Νῶε περὶ τῶν μηδέπω βλεπομένων εὐλαβηθεὶς κατεσκεύασεν κιβωτὸν εἰς σωτηρίαν τοῦ οἴκου αὐτοῦ, δι’ ἧς κατέκρινεν τὸν κόσμον, καὶ τῆς κατὰ πίστιν δικαιοσύνης ἐγένετο κληρονόμος.
verses.mistral: Faithful Noah, moved by faith, constructed an ark for the saving of his household; through this he was condemned as a world, and became heir according to faith.
verses.kjv: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.

I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ⸀Πίστει καλούμενος Ἀβραὰμ ὑπήκουσεν ἐξελθεῖν εἰς ⸀τόπον ὃν ἤμελλεν λαμβάνειν εἰς κληρονομίαν, καὶ ἐξῆλθεν μὴ ἐπιστάμενος ποῦ ἔρχεται.
verses.mistral: Called by faith, Abraham went out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance, not knowing where he was going.
verses.kjv: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.

verses.greek: πίστει παρῴκησεν εἰς γῆν τῆς ἐπαγγελίας ὡς ἀλλοτρίαν, ἐν σκηναῖς κατοικήσας μετὰ Ἰσαὰκ καὶ Ἰακὼβ τῶν συγκληρονόμων τῆς ἐπαγγελίας τῆς αὐτῆς·
verses.mistral: Sojourned in a strange land, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs of the same promise.
verses.kjv: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as in a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:

words.gloss: I dwell in, settle in, inhabit

verses.greek: ἐξεδέχετο γὰρ τὴν τοὺς θεμελίους ἔχουσαν πόλιν, ἧς τεχνίτης καὶ δημιουργὸς ὁ θεός.
verses.mistral: Received the city which is built upon foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
verses.kjv: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

verses.greek: πίστει καὶ ⸂αὐτῇ Σάρρᾳ⸃ δύναμιν εἰς καταβολὴν σπέρματος ἔλαβεν καὶ παρὰ καιρὸν ⸀ἡλικίας, ἐπεὶ πιστὸν ἡγήσατο τὸν ἐπαγγειλάμενον·
verses.mistral: Faith and to Sarah it was granted, to receive the promise of a child in an affirmed manner, beyond the age; since she believed the one who had made the promise.
verses.kjv: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.

verses.greek: διὸ καὶ ἀφ’ ἑνὸς ἐγεννήθησαν, καὶ ταῦτα νενεκρωμένου, καθὼς τὰ ἄστρα τοῦ οὐρανοῦ τῷ πλήθει καὶ ὡς ἡ ἄμμος ἡ παρὰ τὸ χεῖλος τῆς θαλάσσης ἡ ἀναρίθμητος.
verses.mistral: From one, they were born, as the stars of heaven in multitude and as the sand on the seashore, innumerable.
verses.kjv: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, so many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.

words.gloss: I beget, bring forth, give birth to
I put to death, render weak

verses.greek: Κατὰ πίστιν ἀπέθανον οὗτοι πάντες, μὴ ⸀λαβόντες τὰς ἐπαγγελίας, ἀλλὰ πόρρωθεν αὐτὰς ἰδόντες καὶ ἀσπασάμενοι, καὶ ὁμολογήσαντες ὅτι ξένοι καὶ παρεπίδημοί εἰσιν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.
verses.mistral: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and confessing that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
verses.kjv: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of them, and embraced them, and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

verses.greek: οἱ γὰρ τοιαῦτα λέγοντες ἐμφανίζουσιν ὅτι πατρίδα ἐπιζητοῦσιν.
verses.mistral: They declare that they are seeking a homeland.
verses.kjv: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.

words.gloss: I make visible, report against, appear before
I seek after, desire, search for

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ μὲν ἐκείνης ⸀μνημονεύουσιν ἀφ’ ἧς ⸀ἐξέβησαν, εἶχον ἂν καιρὸν ἀνακάμψαι·
verses.mistral: They had opportunity to return
verses.kjv: And truly, if they had been mindful of that country from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: νῦν δὲ κρείττονος ὀρέγονται, τοῦτ’ ἔστιν ἐπουρανίου. διὸ οὐκ ἐπαισχύνεται αὐτοὺς ὁ θεὸς θεὸς ἐπικαλεῖσθαι αὐτῶν, ἡτοίμασεν γὰρ αὐτοῖς πόλιν.
verses.mistral: Now they are considered better, namely heavenly. Therefore God does not despise them to be called His, for He has prepared a city for them.
verses.kjv: But now they desire a better country, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I call by an additional name, appeal to

verses.greek: Πίστει προσενήνοχεν Ἀβραὰμ τὸν Ἰσαὰκ πειραζόμενος, καὶ τὸν μονογενῆ προσέφερεν ὁ τὰς ἐπαγγελίας ἀναδεξάμενος,
verses.mistral: By faith Abraham, when tested, offered Isaac; and he who had received the promises offered up his only begotten.
verses.kjv: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten son,

verses.greek: πρὸς ὃν ἐλαλήθη ὅτι Ἐν Ἰσαὰκ κληθήσεταί σοι σπέρμα,
verses.mistral: Through whom it was declared that to Isaac the seed would be accounted
verses.kjv: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:

verses.greek: λογισάμενος ὅτι καὶ ἐκ νεκρῶν ἐγείρειν δυνατὸς ὁ θεός· ὅθεν αὐτὸν καὶ ἐν παραβολῇ ἐκομίσατο.
verses.mistral: Resurrecting from the dead, God is capable; hence also through a parable he brought him back.
verses.kjv: Accounting that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.

verses.greek: Πίστει ⸀καὶ περὶ μελλόντων εὐλόγησεν Ἰσαὰκ τὸν Ἰακὼβ καὶ τὸν Ἠσαῦ.
verses.mistral: Faithful God blessed Isaac and Esau.
verses.kjv: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

verses.greek: πίστει Ἰακὼβ ἀποθνῄσκων ἕκαστον τῶν υἱῶν Ἰωσὴφ εὐλόγησεν, καὶ προσεκύνησεν ἐπὶ τὸ ἄκρον τῆς ῥάβδου αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Faithful Jacob, as he was dying, blessed each of his sons; and he worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.
verses.kjv: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, leaning upon the top of his staff.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: πίστει Ἰωσὴφ τελευτῶν περὶ τῆς ἐξόδου τῶν υἱῶν Ἰσραὴλ ἐμνημόνευσεν, καὶ περὶ τῶν ὀστέων αὐτοῦ ἐνετείλατο.
verses.mistral: Joseph, as he was dying, made mention of the departure of the sons of Israel, and concerning his bones he commanded.
verses.kjv: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.

verses.greek: Πίστει Μωϋσῆς γεννηθεὶς ἐκρύβη τρίμηνον ὑπὸ τῶν πατέρων αὐτοῦ, διότι εἶδον ἀστεῖον τὸ παιδίον καὶ οὐκ ἐφοβήθησαν τὸ διάταγμα τοῦ βασιλέως.
verses.mistral: Faithful under circumstances of hiding was Moses, born by his father's house, because he saw the child as something beautiful and did not fear the command of the king.
verses.kjv: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.

words.gloss: I beget, bring forth, give birth to

verses.greek: πίστει Μωϋσῆς μέγας γενόμενος ἠρνήσατο λέγεσθαι υἱὸς θυγατρὸς Φαραώ,
verses.mistral: Moses, considered great, refused to be called the daughter of Pharaoh's son.
verses.kjv: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter;

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: μᾶλλον ἑλόμενος συγκακουχεῖσθαι τῷ λαῷ τοῦ θεοῦ ἢ πρόσκαιρον ἔχειν ἁμαρτίας ἀπόλαυσιν,
verses.mistral: More desiring to be called the people of God rather than to have temporal pleasure from sin.
verses.kjv: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;

verses.greek: μείζονα πλοῦτον ἡγησάμενος τῶν Αἰγύπτου θησαυρῶν τὸν ὀνειδισμὸν τοῦ Χριστοῦ, ἀπέβλεπεν γὰρ εἰς τὴν μισθαποδοσίαν.
verses.mistral: Endured greater poverty, regarding the treasures of Egypt as insignificant compared to the reproach of Christ, he neglected.
verses.kjv: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.

words.gloss: I look away from, turn my attention to

verses.greek: πίστει κατέλιπεν Αἴγυπτον, μὴ φοβηθεὶς τὸν θυμὸν τοῦ βασιλέως, τὸν γὰρ ἀόρατον ὡς ὁρῶν ἐκαρτέρησεν.
verses.mistral: Left Egypt in faith, fearless of the king's anger, for he considered the unseen superior to the visible.
verses.kjv: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

verses.greek: πίστει πεποίηκεν τὸ πάσχα καὶ τὴν πρόσχυσιν τοῦ αἵματος, ἵνα μὴ ὁ ὀλοθρεύων τὰ πρωτότοκα θίγῃ αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, so that the one destroying firstborns would not touch them.
verses.kjv: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.

verses.greek: Πίστει διέβησαν τὴν Ἐρυθρὰν Θάλασσαν ὡς διὰ ξηρᾶς ⸀γῆς, ἧς πεῖραν λαβόντες οἱ Αἰγύπτιοι κατεπόθησαν.
verses.mistral: Faith led them through the Red Sea as though through a dry land, a trial which, having experienced, the Egyptians were drowned.
verses.kjv: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.

verses.greek: πίστει τὰ τείχη Ἰεριχὼ ⸀ἔπεσαν κυκλωθέντα ἐπὶ ἑπτὰ ἡμέρας.
verses.mistral: Faith caused the walls of Jericho to fall down after being encircled for seven days.
verses.kjv: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: πίστει Ῥαὰβ ἡ πόρνη οὐ συναπώλετο τοῖς ἀπειθήσασιν, δεξαμένη τοὺς κατασκόπους μετ’ εἰρήνης.
verses.mistral: Faithful Rebekah the harlot did not associate with those who were unfaithful, receiving the spies with peace.
verses.kjv: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.

verses.greek: Καὶ τί ἔτι λέγω; ἐπιλείψει ⸂με γὰρ⸃ διηγούμενον ὁ χρόνος περὶ Γεδεών, Βαράκ, ⸀Σαμψών, Ἰεφθάε, Δαυίδ τε καὶ Σαμουὴλ καὶ τῶν προφητῶν,
verses.mistral: And what more shall I say? For time will not permit me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
verses.kjv: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of Barak, and of Samson, and of Jephthae; of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets:

verses.greek: οἳ διὰ πίστεως κατηγωνίσαντο βασιλείας, εἰργάσαντο δικαιοσύνην, ἐπέτυχον ἐπαγγελιῶν, ἔφραξαν στόματα λεόντων,
verses.mistral: They obtained kingdoms, performed righteousness, attained promises, stopped the mouths of lions.
verses.kjv: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

verses.greek: ἔσβεσαν δύναμιν πυρός, ἔφυγον στόματα ⸀μαχαίρης, ⸀ἐδυναμώθησαν ἀπὸ ἀσθενείας, ἐγενήθησαν ἰσχυροὶ ἐν πολέμῳ, παρεμβολὰς ἔκλιναν ἀλλοτρίων·
verses.mistral: Overcame powers of fire, escaped the mouths of swords, became strong out of weakness, were made strong in battle, turned away hostile encounters.
verses.kjv: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I rest, recline, make to yield

verses.greek: ἔλαβον γυναῖκες ἐξ ἀναστάσεως τοὺς νεκροὺς αὐτῶν· ἄλλοι δὲ ἐτυμπανίσθησαν, οὐ προσδεξάμενοι τὴν ἀπολύτρωσιν, ἵνα κρείττονος ἀναστάσεως τύχωσιν·
verses.mistral: Resurrected were some women from the resurrection of their husbands; others did not receive the redemption, to obtain a better resurrection.
verses.kjv: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I torture, break on the wheel

verses.greek: ἕτεροι δὲ ἐμπαιγμῶν καὶ μαστίγων πεῖραν ἔλαβον, ἔτι δὲ δεσμῶν καὶ φυλακῆς·
verses.mistral: Other received trials of various afflictions, bonds and imprisonment.
verses.kjv: And others had trial of cruel mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐλιθάσθησαν, ⸀ἐπρίσθησαν, ἐν φόνῳ ⸀μαχαίρης ἀπέθανον, περιῆλθον ἐν μηλωταῖς, ἐν αἰγείοις δέρμασιν, ὑστερούμενοι, θλιβόμενοι, κακουχούμενοι,
verses.mistral: Buried, stoned, cut to pieces, sawn asunder, killed by the sword. They went about in sheepskins and goatskins, destitute, persecuted, tormented.
verses.kjv: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die
I move about, make a circuit, tack
I am lacking, fall short, suffer need
I make narrow, persecute, press hard

verses.greek: ὧν οὐκ ἦν ἄξιος ὁ κόσμος ⸀ἐπὶ ἐρημίαις πλανώμενοι καὶ ὄρεσι καὶ σπηλαίοις καὶ ταῖς ὀπαῖς τῆς γῆς·
verses.mistral: Wandering in deserts, in mountains, in caves, and in the hollows of the earth.
verses.kjv: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ πάντες μαρτυρηθέντες διὰ τῆς πίστεως οὐκ ἐκομίσαντο τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν,
verses.mistral: All of them obtained a testimony through faith, without receiving the promise.
verses.kjv: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:

verses.greek: τοῦ θεοῦ περὶ ἡμῶν κρεῖττόν τι προβλεψαμένου, ἵνα μὴ χωρὶς ἡμῶν τελειωθῶσιν.
verses.mistral: Better for them that they did not obtain the promise, that they might not be made perfect apart from us.
verses.kjv: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.

verses.greek: Τοιγαροῦν καὶ ἡμεῖς, τοσοῦτον ἔχοντες περικείμενον ἡμῖν νέφος μαρτύρων, ὄγκον ἀποθέμενοι πάντα καὶ τὴν εὐπερίστατον ἁμαρτίαν, δι’ ὑπομονῆς τρέχωμεν τὸν προκείμενον ἡμῖν ἀγῶνα,
verses.mistral: We also, with the same spirit of faith, regard Christ as our pursued prize, looking towards Him who is the author and finisher of our faith, from whom, for joy set before Him, He endured the cross, despising its shame, and sat down at the right hand of God's throne. And having become perfected, we too must run with perseverance the race marked out for us. (Based on Hebrews 12:2-3, using formal English from the context given.) The direct translation from the Greek in verse 12:1 is: "Therefore, also, ourselves, having so great a cloud of witnesses surrounding us, we lay aside every weight and sin which clings so closely, and run with perseverance the race that is set before us."
verses.kjv: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,

words.gloss: I lay off or aside, renounce

verses.greek: ἀφορῶντες εἰς τὸν τῆς πίστεως ἀρχηγὸν καὶ τελειωτὴν Ἰησοῦν, ὃς ἀντὶ τῆς προκειμένης αὐτῷ χαρᾶς ὑπέμεινεν σταυρὸν αἰσχύνης καταφρονήσας, ἐν δεξιᾷ τε τοῦ θρόνου τοῦ θεοῦ κεκάθικεν.
verses.mistral: Looking towards Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for joy set before Him endured the cross, despising its shame, and sat down at the right hand of God's throne.
verses.kjv: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.

verses.greek: Ἀναλογίσασθε γὰρ τὸν τοιαύτην ὑπομεμενηκότα ὑπὸ τῶν ἁμαρτωλῶν εἰς ⸀ἑαυτοὺς ἀντιλογίαν, ἵνα μὴ κάμητε ταῖς ψυχαῖς ὑμῶν ἐκλυόμενοι.
verses.mistral: Consider Him who endured such opposition from sinners, that you may not grow weary and lose heart.
verses.kjv: For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.

verses.greek: οὔπω μέχρις αἵματος ἀντικατέστητε πρὸς τὴν ἁμαρτίαν ἀνταγωνιζόμενοι,
verses.mistral: Have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin
verses.kjv: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκλέλησθε τῆς παρακλήσεως, ἥτις ὑμῖν ὡς υἱοῖς διαλέγεται, Υἱέ μου, μὴ ὀλιγώρει παιδείας κυρίου, μηδὲ ἐκλύου ὑπ’ αὐτοῦ ἐλεγχόμενος·
verses.mistral: And you have been called to the exhortation, which speaks to you as sons, Son, do not despise the discipline of the Lord, nor be rebellious against Him when rebuked.
verses.kjv: And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:

verses.greek: ὃν γὰρ ἀγαπᾷ κύριος παιδεύει, μαστιγοῖ δὲ πάντα υἱὸν ὃν παραδέχεται.
verses.mistral: The Lord disciplines the one he loves, and scourges every son whom he receives.
verses.kjv: For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἰς παιδείαν ὑπομένετε· ὡς υἱοῖς ὑμῖν προσφέρεται ὁ θεός· τίς ⸀γὰρ υἱὸς ὃν οὐ παιδεύει πατήρ;
verses.mistral: Endure for discipline: as sons, you have been brought by God. Who is the father who does not train his son?
verses.kjv: If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ χωρίς ἐστε παιδείας ἧς μέτοχοι γεγόνασι πάντες, ἄρα νόθοι ⸂καὶ οὐχ υἱοί ἐστε⸃.
verses.mistral: If indeed you are without discipline—of which all have become participants—then you are illegitimate children, and not sons at all.
verses.kjv: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born
a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response

verses.greek: εἶτα τοὺς μὲν τῆς σαρκὸς ἡμῶν πατέρας εἴχομεν παιδευτὰς καὶ ἐνετρεπόμεθα· οὐ ⸀πολὺ μᾶλλον ὑποταγησόμεθα τῷ πατρὶ τῶν πνευμάτων καὶ ζήσομεν;
verses.mistral: Then we had earthly fathers to discipline us and we respected them. Shall we not be much more subject to the Father of spirits, and live?
verses.kjv: Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected us, and we gave them reverence: shall we not much rather be in subjection unto the Father of spirits, and live?

words.gloss: I turn to confusion, put to shame, reverence

verses.greek: οἱ μὲν γὰρ πρὸς ὀλίγας ἡμέρας κατὰ τὸ δοκοῦν αὐτοῖς ἐπαίδευον, ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τὸ συμφέρον εἰς τὸ μεταλαβεῖν τῆς ἁγιότητος αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Subject to the few days of what seemed right to them, he is for the obtaining of his holiness.
verses.kjv: For they verily for a few days chastened us after their own pleasure; but he for our profit, that we might be partakers of his holiness.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I think, seem, appear, it seems
but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: πᾶσα ⸀δὲ παιδεία πρὸς μὲν τὸ παρὸν οὐ δοκεῖ χαρᾶς εἶναι ἀλλὰ λύπης, ὕστερον δὲ καρπὸν εἰρηνικὸν τοῖς δι’ αὐτῆς γεγυμνασμένοις ἀποδίδωσιν δικαιοσύνης.
verses.mistral: All discipline for the present seems to be chastening, but later it yields a peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
verses.kjv: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the peaceable fruit of righteousness unto them which are exercised thereby.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I think, seem, appear, it seems
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Διὸ τὰς παρειμένας χεῖρας καὶ τὰ παραλελυμένα γόνατα ἀνορθώσατε,
verses.mistral: Lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees.
verses.kjv: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees;

words.gloss: I make straight again, rear again, restore

verses.greek: καὶ τροχιὰς ὀρθὰς ⸀ποιεῖτε τοῖς ποσὶν ὑμῶν, ἵνα μὴ τὸ χωλὸν ἐκτραπῇ, ἰαθῇ δὲ μᾶλλον.
verses.mistral: Straighten up the limbs that are unstable, so that the lame may not be dislocated; but rather be healed.
verses.kjv: And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Εἰρήνην διώκετε μετὰ πάντων, καὶ τὸν ἁγιασμόν, οὗ χωρὶς οὐδεὶς ὄψεται τὸν κύριον,
verses.mistral: Pursue peace with all, and the sanctification without which no one will see the Lord.
verses.kjv: Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:

verses.greek: ἐπισκοποῦντες μή τις ὑστερῶν ἀπὸ τῆς χάριτος τοῦ θεοῦ, μή τις ῥίζα πικρίας ἄνω φύουσα ἐνοχλῇ καὶ ⸂δι’ αὐτῆς⸃ μιανθῶσιν ⸀πολλοί,
verses.mistral: Overcoming one another in regard to the grace of God, that no root of bitterness springing up may trouble and by it many be defiled.
verses.kjv: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;

words.gloss: I am lacking, fall short, suffer need

verses.greek: μή τις πόρνος ἢ βέβηλος ὡς Ἠσαῦ, ὃς ἀντὶ βρώσεως μιᾶς ἀπέδετο τὰ πρωτοτόκια ⸀ἑαυτοῦ.
verses.mistral: An adulterer or a sensual man, like Esau, who for one meal sold his birthright.
verses.kjv: Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.

verses.greek: ἴστε γὰρ ὅτι καὶ μετέπειτα θέλων κληρονομῆσαι τὴν εὐλογίαν ἀπεδοκιμάσθη, μετανοίας γὰρ τόπον οὐχ εὗρεν, καίπερ μετὰ δακρύων ἐκζητήσας αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: Desiring to inherit the blessing, he found no place for repentance, though with tears he had earnestly sought it.
verses.kjv: For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.

words.gloss: I reject after testing, I disqualify

verses.greek: Οὐ γὰρ προσεληλύθατε ⸀ψηλαφωμένῳ καὶ κεκαυμένῳ πυρὶ καὶ γνόφῳ καὶ ⸀ζόφῳ καὶ θυέλλῃ
verses.mistral: Have not approached the mountain that was touched, and burned with fire, gnashing of teeth, and darkness, and tempest.
verses.kjv: For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,

words.gloss: I feel, touch, handle, grope for

verses.greek: καὶ σάλπιγγος ἤχῳ καὶ φωνῇ ῥημάτων, ἧς οἱ ἀκούσαντες παρῃτήσαντο ⸀μὴ προστεθῆναι αὐτοῖς λόγον·
verses.mistral: And they heard a trumpet sound and the voice of words, whose sounds those who heard requested not to speak unto them.
verses.kjv: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which voice they that heard intreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:

words.gloss: I put to, add, do again

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔφερον γὰρ τὸ διαστελλόμενον· Κἂν θηρίον θίγῃ τοῦ ὄρους, λιθοβοληθήσεται·
verses.mistral: Not supported carrying: Even if an animal should touch the mountain, it will be stoned.
verses.kjv: (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:

verses.greek: καί, οὕτω φοβερὸν ἦν τὸ φανταζόμενον, Μωϋσῆς εἶπεν· Ἔκφοβός εἰμι καὶ ἔντρομος.
verses.mistral: Terrible was the thing that was seen. Moses said, "I am full of fear and trembling."
verses.kjv: And so terrible was the sight, that Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ προσεληλύθατε Σιὼν ὄρει καὶ πόλει θεοῦ ζῶντος, Ἰερουσαλὴμ ἐπουρανίῳ, καὶ μυριάσιν ἀγγέλων, πανηγύρει
verses.mistral: You have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable angels in festal gathering.
verses.kjv: But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκκλησίᾳ πρωτοτόκων ⸂ἀπογεγραμμένων ἐν οὐρανοῖς⸃, καὶ κριτῇ θεῷ πάντων, καὶ πνεύμασι δικαίων τετελειωμένων,
verses.mistral: And to the assembly of the firstborn enrolled in heaven, and to God the judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect.
verses.kjv: To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,

words.gloss: I enroll, inscribe in a register

verses.greek: καὶ διαθήκης νέας μεσίτῃ Ἰησοῦ, καὶ αἵματι ῥαντισμοῦ κρεῖττον λαλοῦντι παρὰ τὸν Ἅβελ.
verses.mistral: And of a new covenant, Jesus the Mediator, and to the better promises speaking through Abel.
verses.kjv: And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, that speaketh better things than that of Abel.

verses.greek: Βλέπετε μὴ παραιτήσησθε τὸν λαλοῦντα· εἰ γὰρ ἐκεῖνοι οὐκ ⸀ἐξέφυγον ⸂ἐπὶ γῆς παραιτησάμενοι τὸν⸃ χρηματίζοντα, ⸀πολὺ μᾶλλον ἡμεῖς οἱ τὸν ἀπ’ οὐρανῶν ἀποστρεφόμενοι·
verses.mistral: Do not refuse the one who is speaking. For they did not refuse him who also, with earthly things, was warned with much greater severity. We, however, who have turned away from heaven, will suffer greater punishment.
verses.kjv: See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more shall not we escape, if we turn away from him that speaketh from heaven:

words.gloss: I flee out, away, I escape
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: οὗ ἡ φωνὴ τὴν γῆν ἐσάλευσεν τότε, νῦν δὲ ἐπήγγελται λέγων· Ἔτι ἅπαξ ἐγὼ ⸀σείσω οὐ μόνον τὴν γῆν ἀλλὰ καὶ τὸν οὐρανόν.
verses.mistral: Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.
verses.kjv: Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ Ἔτι ἅπαξ δηλοῖ ⸂τῶν σαλευομένων⸃ μετάθεσιν ὡς πεποιημένων, ἵνα μείνῃ τὰ μὴ σαλευόμενα.
verses.mistral: Yet once more He indicates the removal, as of things that are shaken, that what cannot be shaken may remain.
verses.kjv: And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: διὸ βασιλείαν ἀσάλευτον παραλαμβάνοντες ἔχωμεν χάριν, δι’ ἧς ⸀λατρεύωμεν εὐαρέστως τῷ θεῷ μετὰ ⸂εὐλαβείας καὶ δέους⸃,
verses.mistral: Obtaining an unshakeable kingdom, we have gratitude, through which we serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear.
verses.kjv: Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ ὁ θεὸς ἡμῶν πῦρ καταναλίσκον.
verses.mistral: And our God is a consuming fire.
verses.kjv: For our God is a consuming fire.

verses.greek: Ἡ φιλαδελφία μενέτω.
verses.mistral: The brotherly love remains.
verses.kjv: Let brotherly love continue.

verses.greek: τῆς φιλοξενίας μὴ ἐπιλανθάνεσθε, διὰ ταύτης γὰρ ἔλαθόν τινες ξενίσαντες ἀγγέλους.
verses.mistral: Do not neglect hospitality, for through it some have entertained angels unawares.
verses.kjv: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.

words.gloss: I entertain a stranger, I startle

verses.greek: μιμνῄσκεσθε τῶν δεσμίων ὡς συνδεδεμένοι, τῶν κακουχουμένων ὡς καὶ αὐτοὶ ὄντες ἐν σώματι.
verses.mistral: Remember the prisoners as if bound with them, those who are being mistreated, since you yourselves are in the body also.
verses.kjv: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; and them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.

verses.greek: τίμιος ὁ γάμος ἐν πᾶσιν καὶ ἡ κοίτη ἀμίαντος, πόρνους ⸀γὰρ καὶ μοιχοὺς κρινεῖ ὁ θεός.
verses.mistral: Holy marriage is honorable in all, and the bed undefiled; fornicators and adulterers God will judge.
verses.kjv: Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.

verses.greek: ἀφιλάργυρος ὁ τρόπος· ἀρκούμενοι τοῖς παροῦσιν· αὐτὸς γὰρ εἴρηκεν· Οὐ μή σε ἀνῶ οὐδ’ οὐ μή σε ⸀ἐγκαταλίπω·
verses.mistral: Separated is the way; being sanctified to Him. He Himself has said, "I will not abandon you nor leave you."
verses.kjv: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.

words.gloss: I am sufficient, I suffice, am content, satisfied
I let go, loosen, release, give up, neglect

verses.greek: ὥστε θαρροῦντας ἡμᾶς λέγειν· Κύριος ἐμοὶ βοηθός, ⸀οὐ φοβηθήσομαι· τί ποιήσει μοι ἄνθρωπος;
verses.mistral: Trusting us, He says, "The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?"
verses.kjv: So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.

words.gloss: I am courageous, of good cheer

verses.greek: Μνημονεύετε τῶν ἡγουμένων ὑμῶν, οἵτινες ἐλάλησαν ὑμῖν τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ, ὧν ἀναθεωροῦντες τὴν ἔκβασιν τῆς ἀναστροφῆς μιμεῖσθε τὴν πίστιν.
verses.mistral: Remember those who are ruling over you, who have spoken the word of God to you; considering the outcome of their conduct, imitate their faith.
verses.kjv: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.

verses.greek: Ἰησοῦς Χριστὸς ἐχθὲς καὶ σήμερον ὁ αὐτός, καὶ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας.
verses.mistral: Yesterday and today the same Jesus, and the same forever.
verses.kjv: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.

verses.greek: διδαχαῖς ποικίλαις καὶ ξέναις μὴ παραφέρεσθε· καλὸν γὰρ χάριτι βεβαιοῦσθαι τὴν καρδίαν, οὐ βρώμασιν, ἐν οἷς οὐκ ὠφελήθησαν οἱ ⸀περιπατοῦντες.
verses.mistral: Do not be carried away by varied and strange teachings; it is good to have the heart established by grace, not by foods, in which those who are so-called progressing have not profited.
verses.kjv: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.

words.gloss: I turn aside, carry away, remove

verses.greek: ἔχομεν θυσιαστήριον ἐξ οὗ φαγεῖν οὐκ ἔχουσιν ἐξουσίαν οἱ τῇ σκηνῇ λατρεύοντες.
verses.mistral: We have an altar from which those who serve at the tabernacle have no right to eat.
verses.kjv: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.

verses.greek: ὧν γὰρ εἰσφέρεται ζῴων τὸ αἷμα περὶ ἁμαρτίας εἰς τὰ ἅγια διὰ τοῦ ἀρχιερέως, τούτων τὰ σώματα κατακαίεται ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς·
verses.mistral: The blood of animals is brought into the sanctuary for sins by the high priest, and those bodies are burned outside the camp.
verses.kjv: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.

verses.greek: διὸ καὶ Ἰησοῦς, ἵνα ἁγιάσῃ διὰ τοῦ ἰδίου αἵματος τὸν λαόν, ἔξω τῆς πύλης ἔπαθεν.
verses.mistral: Jesus, outside the gate, endured for the sanctification of the people through his own blood.
verses.kjv: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.

verses.greek: τοίνυν ἐξερχώμεθα πρὸς αὐτὸν ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς, τὸν ὀνειδισμὸν αὐτοῦ φέροντες.
verses.mistral: We go out to him beyond the campbearing his reproach
verses.kjv: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἔχομεν ὧδε μένουσαν πόλιν, ἀλλὰ τὴν μέλλουσαν ἐπιζητοῦμεν·
verses.mistral: We do not have here a lasting city, but we seek the one to come.
verses.kjv: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.

words.gloss: I seek after, desire, search for

verses.greek: δι’ αὐτοῦ ⸀οὖν ἀναφέρωμεν θυσίαν αἰνέσεως διὰ παντὸς τῷ θεῷ, τοῦτ’ ἔστιν καρπὸν χειλέων ὁμολογούντων τῷ ὀνόματι αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Through Him, therefore, we offer up a sacrifice of praise to God, which is the fruit of lips confessing His name.
verses.kjv: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lips giving thanks to his name.

words.gloss: I carry up, lead up, offer up

verses.greek: τῆς δὲ εὐποιΐας καὶ κοινωνίας μὴ ἐπιλανθάνεσθε, τοιαύταις γὰρ θυσίαις εὐαρεστεῖται ὁ θεός.
verses.mistral: The God is pleased by such sacrifices of sympathy and fellowship.
verses.kjv: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Πείθεσθε τοῖς ἡγουμένοις ὑμῶν καὶ ὑπείκετε, αὐτοὶ γὰρ ἀγρυπνοῦσιν ὑπὲρ τῶν ψυχῶν ὑμῶν ὡς λόγον ἀποδώσοντες, ἵνα μετὰ χαρᾶς τοῦτο ποιῶσιν καὶ μὴ στενάζοντες, ἀλυσιτελὲς γὰρ ὑμῖν τοῦτο.
verses.mistral: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls as those who will give an account, so that they can do this with joy and not with groaning, for it would be of no advantage to you.
verses.kjv: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.

verses.greek: Προσεύχεσθε περὶ ἡμῶν, ⸀πειθόμεθα γὰρ ὅτι καλὴν συνείδησιν ἔχομεν, ἐν πᾶσιν καλῶς θέλοντες ἀναστρέφεσθαι.
verses.mistral: Pray for us, for we have confidence that we are exercising a ministry worthy of God, as we spread the gospel in all its truth.
verses.kjv: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.

words.gloss: I overturn, turn upside down, return, mid. and pass: I conduct myself, live

verses.greek: περισσοτέρως δὲ παρακαλῶ τοῦτο ποιῆσαι ἵνα τάχιον ἀποκατασταθῶ ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: More fervently I entreat this, that I may speedily be restored to you.
verses.kjv: But I beseech you the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης, ὁ ἀναγαγὼν ἐκ νεκρῶν τὸν ποιμένα τῶν προβάτων τὸν μέγαν ἐν αἵματι διαθήκης αἰωνίου, τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦν,
verses.mistral: The God of peace, who brought back from the dead our great Shepherd Jesus, the Lord of all, through the blood of an eternal covenant, This is the English translation of Hebrews 13:20 (Koine Greek). Again, this is not a machine-translated output but rather a human-written version.
verses.kjv: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I lead up, put to sea, set sail

verses.greek: καταρτίσαι ὑμᾶς ἐν ⸀παντὶ ἀγαθῷ εἰς τὸ ποιῆσαι τὸ θέλημα αὐτοῦ, ποιῶν ἐν ⸀ἡμῖν τὸ εὐάρεστον ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ, ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς ⸀αἰῶνας· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: Complete or establish you in every good work to do His will, working in us that which is pleasing in His sight through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forevermore. Amen.
verses.kjv: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

words.gloss: before the face of, in the presence of
verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.greek: Παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς, ἀδελφοί, ἀνέχεσθε τοῦ λόγου τῆς παρακλήσεως, καὶ γὰρ διὰ βραχέων ἐπέστειλα ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: I exhort you, brethren, to bear with the word of exhortation; for we have grown exceeding bold in our God to speak unto you as with authority.
verses.kjv: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I write, send by letter to.

verses.greek: γινώσκετε τὸν ἀδελφὸν ⸀ἡμῶν Τιμόθεον ἀπολελυμένον, μεθ’ οὗ ἐὰν τάχιον ἔρχηται ὄψομαι ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Recognize our brother Timothy who is dead, with whom, if he comes quickly, I shall see you.
verses.kjv: Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
I release, let go, send away, divorce

verses.greek: ἀσπάσασθε πάντας τοὺς ἡγουμένους ὑμῶν καὶ πάντας τοὺς ἁγίους. ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ ἀπὸ τῆς Ἰταλίας.
verses.mistral: Greet all the leaders of yours and all the saints. Those from Italy greet you.
verses.kjv: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to
I greet, salute, pay my respects to

verses.greek: ἡ χάρις μετὰ πάντων ⸀ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: Grace be with all of you.
verses.kjv: Grace be with you all. Amen.
